title: Legal Compliance Check
learning_objectives: Research applicable cybercrime legislation for penetration testing
scenario: Your company has been asked to perform a penetration test for a bank in Botswana. Before any technical work, the legal team needs you to prepare all pre-engagement documentation. Get this wrong and the engagement, and possibly your career, is over.

scenario_context.setting: Bank in Botswana requesting a penetration test
scenario_context.relevance: Unauthorised testing is a criminal offence in most African jurisdictions. Professional pentesters must understand and comply with local legislation.

subtasks.title: Legal Research
subtasks.description: Research Botswana's Cybercrime Act and relevant legislation. Document specific clauses applying to penetration testing.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Find the Cybercrime and Computer Related Crimes Act. Identify clauses on: unauthorised access, interception, data interference. Note what authorisation is needed to legally conduct testing.
subtasks.evidence_required: Legal research summary with clauses cited

subtasks.title: Scope Document
subtasks.description: Create a complete scope document for the bank engagement using the provided template.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Include: target systems (IPs, domains, applications), in-scope activities, out-of-scope systems, testing window, testing types permitted, data handling requirements.

subtasks.title: Rules of Engagement
subtasks.description: Draft rules covering testing hours, communication protocols, escalation procedures, data handling, and reporting.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Cover: testing hours, communication channels, escalation for critical findings, data handling and destruction, reporting timeline, re-testing policy.

subtasks.title: Authorisation Checklist
subtasks.description: Create a reusable pre-engagement checklist for legal compliance on future engagements.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Make it universal: applicable to any African jurisdiction. Include: legal research, written authorisation, scope definition, rules of engagement, emergency contacts, data handling, insurance verification.

reflection_prompt: You discover a critical vulnerability during a pentest, but it's in a system that's explicitly out of scope. What do you do? Walk through the ethical and legal considerations.

quiz.question: Testing without written authorisation is:
options: A criminal offence in most jurisdictions regardless of intent
Only illegal if you cause damage
Legal if you're a certified professional
quiz.explanation: Unauthorised access to computer systems is a criminal offence in virtually all jurisdictions, regardless of intent or outcome.

quiz.question: A scope document protects the tester by:
options: Defining exactly what is authorised, creating a legal defence if questions arise
quiz.explanation: The scope document defines the authorised boundaries. If a tester stays within scope, the document serves as legal defence.

mentor_notes: Legal accuracy is non-negotiable. The checklist should be genuinely reusable. Evaluate professionalism — these documents would be reviewed by lawyers.
